# Offensive security with Kali, across every substrate 🔴

**Goal.** Stand up Kali as a chroot, a VM, a built image, and a PXE install — ending in an agentic LLM that drives real Kali tools.

**Audience:** 🔒 authorized targets only — throwaway/isolated networks  
**Effort:** ⏱ half-day+  
**Prerequisites:** [Containers from first principles](path-container-internals.md) · [Chroot → running system: the cross-phase bridges](path-chroot-to-system.md)

| # | Lab | You'll learn | Checkpoint (observable) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [`vm-kali-amd64.toml`](../vm-kali-amd64.toml) | the prebuilt Kali rolling VM — the quickest way in. | boot Kali; `nmap` a loopback target returns a scan. |
| 2 | [`kali-nonroot-chroot`](../kali-nonroot-chroot/) 🔒 | a non-root Kali chroot + a top-10 tool slice (contrib/non-free + archive keyring). | `nmap`/`sqlmap` run as the non-root `kali` user; root is locked (`su` fails). |
| 3 | [`kali-vm-builder`](../kali-vm-builder/) 🔒 | Kali's official `kali-vm`/`debos` image factory. | build your own QCOW2 (`--headless`); `run-graphical.sh` boots it. |
| 4 | [`kali-preseed-gallery`](../kali-preseed-gallery/) | install Kali unattended via a preseed variant. | a zero-touch variant install completes and reboots into Kali. |
| 5 | [`offsec-awae-vm`](../offsec-awae-vm/) 🔒 | the AWAE (WEB-300) toolset chroot → VM (shared with the chroot-to-system path). | the VM boots with the WEB-300 tooling ready. |
| 6 | [`kali-llm-lab`](../kali-llm-lab/) 🪶 | a rootless Ollama + Open WebUI pod reached over SSH-forward. | Ollama answers a prompt over the forwarded port. |
| 7 | [`kali-llm-desktop-lab`](../kali-llm-desktop-lab/) | the full XFCE VM with the real 5ire GUI + mcp-kali-server — the agentic payoff. | the LLM invokes `nmap` through mcp-kali-server against an isolated target. |
